evals.prompt:
Generate the canonical MIF Level-3 report from the sample findings. The report must carry a REAL falsification verdict (do not author one by hand) — derive it from the falsification gate. Run exactly:

  OUTDIR=$(pwd)
  bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/synthesize-artifact.sh $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/findings general $OUTDIR/artifact.json
  bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/falsify.sh $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/evals/fixtures/raw-finding.json $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/evals/fixtures/evidence.json > $OUTDIR/ff.json
  jq '.extensions.harness.verification' $OUTDIR/ff.json > $OUTDIR/verification.json
  bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/render-artifact.sh $OUTDIR/artifact.json report $OUTDIR/report.md $OUTDIR/verification.json

Confirm the report projects to a valid MIF L3 finding before reporting done.
evals.expected_output: report.md with MIF YAML frontmatter (a urn:mif:report: @id, citations, provenance, extensions.harness.verification with a non-falsified verdict, dimension: synthesis) over a Markdown body with a ## Sources section and no top-level (#) heading; artifact.json valid JSON; the report projects to a valid MIF L3 finding via mif-project.sh.

deterministic_checks.description: the canonical report file was created
frontmatter carries a urn:mif:report: id (the report IS a MIF concept)
frontmatter carries a falsification verification block
the report uses the reserved synthesis dimension token
the body has a ## Sources section
the report projects to a valid MIF L3 finding (same bar as a finding + citation-integrity)

expectations: report.md is a single markdown file: authoritative YAML frontmatter (the MIF concept) over a clean Markdown body, with the title only in the frontmatter.
The verification verdict was taken from the falsification gate output, not invented; it is one of survived/weakened/inconclusive (never falsified for a shipped report).
The report is the canonical MIF Level-3 source of truth and validates against findings.schema.json via mif-project.sh.

evals.prompt:
A falsified report must never ship. Demonstrate the guardrail: synthesize the sample report, but attach a FALSIFIED verdict, and show the report is rejected. Run:

  OUTDIR=$(pwd)
  bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/synthesize-artifact.sh $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/findings general $OUTDIR/artifact.json
  printf '%s' '{"verdict":"falsified","verdict_basis":"Disconfirming search refuted the central claim.","attempted_at":"2026-06-19T12:30:00Z"}' > $OUTDIR/verification.json
  bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/render-artifact.sh $OUTDIR/artifact.json report $OUTDIR/report.md $OUTDIR/verification.json ; echo "render exit: $?"

The render must FAIL (non-zero) and report.md must not be a valid L3 report. Report what happened.
evals.expected_output: render-artifact.sh exits non-zero because a falsified report fails the write-then-validate gate (citation-integrity rejects a falsified verdict); the falsified report does not ship.

deterministic_checks.description: a report carrying a falsified verdict does NOT project to a valid L3 finding (rejected)

expectations: The render step fails closed: a falsified report cannot pass write-then-validate.
The guardrail is the citation-integrity gate, which rejects a falsified verdict — the same rule that protects findings.
The agent reports the failure honestly rather than claiming the report shipped.

evals.prompt:
Render the canonical report using the 'technical' genre to confirm genres are held to L3 (genres are not exempt). Run:

  OUTDIR=$(pwd)
  bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/synthesize-artifact.sh $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/findings technical $OUTDIR/artifact.json
  bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/falsify.sh $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/evals/fixtures/raw-finding.json $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/evals/fixtures/evidence.json > $OUTDIR/ff.json
  jq '.extensions.harness.verification' $OUTDIR/ff.json > $OUTDIR/verification.json
  bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/render-artifact.sh $OUTDIR/artifact.json report $OUTDIR/report.md $OUTDIR/verification.json

Confirm the technical-genre report is valid MIF L3.
evals.expected_output: A technical-genre report.md that still projects to a valid MIF L3 finding — genres shape content but are held to L3, never exempt.

deterministic_checks.description: the artifact records the technical genre
the genre report is held to L3 and validates (genres are not exempt)

expectations: The technical-genre report is rendered through the same report channel and validates at MIF L3.
Exemption is for orthogonal formats (pdf, audio, external-service bodies), never for genres.
The report carries a real, non-falsified verification verdict.
